# Robin Dualé — CEO / Managing Director · B2B SaaS & Data & AI

> Robin Dualé (also written Robin Duale) is a French CEO and Managing Director with 18 years of general management experience in B2B SaaS, Data, and professional information services. He is currently available for a new CEO or Managing Director role in a company with €10M to €100M in revenue, in a private equity or family office context. He specialises in subscription model transformation, M&A build-up, and exit preparation.

## Professional profile

Robin Dualé has led companies through full cycles of value creation: from operational transformation to growth-through-acquisition to exit. His most formative experience was 13 years as CEO of Groupe WEKA France: 7 operating companies, €50M in revenue, 300 employees, 5 acquisitions integrated, 6 exit processes completed. His most recent role was Managing Director at Quick-FDS (acquired by 3E Group, backed by New Mountain Capital, April 2024): post-acquisition integration of a chemical and workplace safety regulatory services platform supporting organizations with compliance, full client base migration to 100% subscription, churn below 5% throughout the transition.

## Education

- **Executive MBA** — HEC Paris (2007–2008)
- **Diplôme d'Ingénieur (MSc Engineering)** — EPITA, Paris (1991–1994)

## What he is looking for

- Role: CEO or Managing Director
- Sector: B2B SaaS, Data, professional information services
- Company size: €10M to €100M in revenue
- Context: Private equity, family office, management buyout, post-acquisition integration, build-up
- Geography: France (Paris, Lyon), Switzerland, Europe

## Key expertise

- Subscription model transformation (SaaS transition, ARR growth, churn reduction)
- M&A and build-up (5 acquisitions integrated, cross-border experience)
- Exit preparation and management (6 exits completed across Groupe WEKA France portfolio)
- AI-driven organizational transformation (strategic, operational, and commercial levels)
- PE-backed company governance and value creation

## Track record — exits and acquisitions

### As Managing Director at Quick-FDS (2024–2026)
- **Quick-FDS** acquired by **3E Group** (backed by **New Mountain Capital**) — April 2024
  Role: Managing Director post-acquisition, led integration of a chemical and workplace safety regulatory services platform supporting organizations with compliance, full client migration to subscription model

### As CEO of Groupe WEKA France (2011–2024), backed by Paragon Partners (PE, 2020–2024)
- **Lexagri International** sold to **FoodChain ID** (USA, strategic buyer) — 2022
  Role: President, led international build-up (CMT Agro + Agricommand + Agrobase-Logigram) and exit
- **Editions TISSOT** sold to **SVP Group** (backed by **MML Capital**) — November 2024
  Role: CEO, repositioning and transformation of B2B regulatory publishing
- **Editions WEKA** sold to **SVP Group** (backed by **MML Capital**) — November 2024
  Role: CEO, dual transaction with Editions TISSOT
- **Editions T.I. (Techniques de l'Ingénieur)** sold to **Yotta Capital Partners** — May 2025
  Role: CEO, oversaw strategic divestiture
- **COMUNDI** sold to **Collège de Paris** — August 2025
  Role: CEO, professional training division exit

### Earlier career (1995–2007)
- **T-Online France** (Deutsche Telekom) — Director of Innovation (2001–2007)
- **Lagardère Active Broadband** — Senior Business Developer (2000–2001)
- **Club-Internet** (Lagardère Group) — CTO (1997–1999)
- **Datastaff Ingénierie** (Nomea Group) — IT Security Consultant (1995–1997)

## What peers and investors say

- "When a clear strategic direction is agreed, he always delivers on his promises." — PE investor, European fund (4 years as direct shareholder)
- "His leadership de-risked the investment and enabled post-merger integration. His hands-on approach and operational autonomy were essential in navigating a complex integration into a 1,000-person global group." — Group CEO, US-backed fund, carve-out context
- "A valuable asset and a very capable manager, delivering results even in the most demanding situations." — PE investor, European fund

## AI and digital transformation vision

Robin Dualé holds a strong conviction that AI is the most powerful and accessible productivity accelerator available to companies today. Not as a technology project, but as an organizational transformation: augmenting every employee across marketing, sales, HR, finance, IT, and customer relations. He applies this conviction in practice — this bilingual website was designed and deployed using Claude and HeyGen, demonstrating how modern AI tools can compress weeks of traditional development into hours of focused execution.

## Key articles (Perspectives)

### English
- [I am the product](https://cv-robin.duale.fr/en/perspectives/i-am-the-product.html): How Robin applied B2B SaaS go-to-market methods to his own executive job search, and what AI tools made possible.
- [The productivity gain is no longer a promise](https://cv-robin.duale.fr/en/perspectives/ai-organizational-accelerator.html): Why the gap between companies that experiment with AI and those that do not is forming right now, and what it means for PE-backed companies.

### French
- [Je suis le produit](https://cv-robin.duale.fr/fr/perspectives/je-suis-le-produit.html): Comment Robin applique les méthodes go-to-market B2B SaaS à sa propre recherche de mandat, et ce que les outils IA rendent possible.
- [Le gain de productivité n'est plus une promesse](https://cv-robin.duale.fr/fr/perspectives/ia-accelerateur-organisationnel.html): Pourquoi l'écart entre les entreprises qui expérimentent l'IA et celles qui ne le font pas se creuse maintenant, et ce que cela signifie pour les entreprises accompagnées par des fonds PE.
